response: {"purpose":"Figure 3a presents pie charts summarising the experimental success outcomes of 209 eCLIP experiments conducted in two human cell lines (K562 and HepG2), categorising results by the degree of amplification (eCT) needed to generate sufficient sequencing library from immunoprecipitated material. This breakdown matters because it transparently quantifies the scalability and reliability of the eCLIP platform at a large-scale, demonstrating that 102 of 209 experiments met quality thresholds and were deposited in the ENCODE repository. By distinguishing experiments according to amplification requirements, the figure also highlights practical considerations—such as input RNA abundance and antibody performance—that influence assay success. Together, these data establish the empirical foundation for the broader claim that eCLIP can be deployed systematically across a large panel of RNA-binding proteins.","title":"eCLIP Experimental Success Rates Across 209 RBP Experiments"}
